jobs.title: Product Owner / Project Manager - LATAM, Remote
jobs.jd_full: The SilverLogic is a custom software development and technology solutions partner specializing in business automation and AI-powered innovation. We design and build tailored platforms that streamline operations, enhance decision-making, and unlock new opportunities for growth. Leveraging advanced technologies such as AI, process automation, IoT, and AR, within an Agile Scrum framework, we transform complex challenges into scalable, meaningful solutions. We are seeking a Product Owner / Project Manager to own client success from concept to delivery. This role combines strategic product leadership with hands-on project management to ensure that each initiative is on vision, on budget, and on time.

jobs.title: Junior Project Coordinator
jobs.jd_full:
C4Media, Inc. is seeking a motivated, fully remote Junior Project Coordinator (contractor) to join our global team. In this role, you will provide essential support for our expanding online learning and certification cohorts.

As a Junior Project Coordinator, you will assist the team in planning and executing our virtual learning programs for software professionals. This is a foundational, entry-level role designed for someone who thrives on organization, clear communication, and the "behind-the-scenes" work.

You will work under the close guidance of our program leads, learning the operational "ins and outs" of professional education in a highly collaborative environment.

What You’ll Do

Working as part of a team, you will assist with the daily operations of our online learning cohorts:

    • Customer Service: Act as a first point of contact for learners. You’ll help distribute onboarding materials, answer inquiries, and follow up to ensure participants have a smooth experience.
    • Logistics Assistance: Help maintain our digital learning environments, including Zoom room administration, Google Drive organization, and coordinating Slack communications.
    • Editorial Pipeline Support: Support the coordination of "Capstone" learning projects by collecting participant bios, tracking deadlines, and sharing information with our internal editorial team.
    • Trainer Coordination: Provide administrative support to facilitators by ensuring session materials are organized and sessions are ready to launch.
    • Data Entry & Tracking: Assist in keeping our databases accurate. You’ll help track attendance, distribute feedback surveys, and compile results for program reports.
    • Ad-Hoc Team Support: Help with research tasks and small operational details, such as tracking costs or coordinating with Finance and Marketing.

 

Job Requirements

 

Professional Experience, Skills & Education:

 

    • Excellent English proficiency (written & spoken) with the ability to communicate professionally with a global audience.
    • A customer-first mindset—you are naturally helpful, patient, and supportive.
    • Sharp attention to detail and exceptional organizational skills.
    • Comfort with remote productivity tools such as Google Workspace and Slack.
    • Technology curiosity: A desire to learn new software. Experience with CRMs, Figma, or basic HTML is a plus, but not required.
    • A recent degree or 1-2 years of experience in an administrative or customer-facing role.
    • A proactive, solution-oriented mindset with a strong willingness to help.

 

Setup Requirements:

 

    • Able to work in a full-time remote position
    • Must reside in Argentina or Brazil
    • Able to work as an independent contractor
    • Able to work in overlap with our core global office hours (9 AM - 1 PM EST)
    • Quiet home office and ability to work comfortably from home
    • Reliable infrastructure: Access to high-speed internet and a modern computer
    • Able and willing to travel to locations in the USA or Europe 1 - 3 times per year, with an average stay of 4-8 days each, to attend our software conferences and annual company meetings

 

Only candidates who submit their applications in English will be considered for this role (including resume).

Why work at C4Media

    • Work from home - always: We are a remote-first and remote-always team who has been successfully operating on a work-from-home basis since 2007. And we have no intention of changing that.
    • Travel the world: C4Media offers an opportunity to travel 3-4 times a year at our expense to NYC, SF, London, and other fun, global locations for conferences & team building. We also got you covered to add a sightseeing day to the end of the trip.
    • Take care of each other: We look out for one another and prioritize respect, fairness, support, and well-being. Check out our core values on our careers page.
    • Learn something new: C4Media’s culture is one of learning & mastering. Everyone has a training and education budget for professional growth every year and is encouraged to use it.
    • Make friends across the world: Be a part of a leading, fast-growing international company and build a network of international friends and colleagues for life.
    • Support wellbeing: In an effort to make physical activity more readily accessible, we offer staff an annual subsidy towards fitness and wellness.
    • Generous paid time off: In addition to 25 paid days off in the 1st year and 30 paid days off in every subsequent year, we provide 1 paid day off for birthdays (or a similar special day) and 2 paid days for continuing education.
    • Mentorship: Receive direct guidance and professional development from experienced project leads.
    • Global Exposure: Interact with tech professionals and world-class trainers daily.
    • Event Experience: Gain a front-row seat to how major international conferences are run, with potential opportunities to support in-person events.

 

Qualified candidates are to submit their applications on our C4Media website.

jobs.title: Senior Product Manager
jobs.jd_full:
At Secureframe, we are not just a company; we are at the forefront of revolutionizing cybersecurity compliance. Recognized as one of the industry’s most innovative and trusted providers, Secureframe has consistently received accolades for our advanced technology solutions and commitment to excellence. With a robust portfolio of products that safeguard thousands of businesses worldwide, we have been featured in major publications such as Forbes’ next billion dollar startups, TechCrunch, and The Wall Street Journal for our transformative impact on the way companies achieve and maintain compliance standards.

As we continue to grow, our mission remains clear: to provide seamless, secure compliance solutions that enable businesses to focus on what they do best. Joining Secureframe means becoming part of a dynamic team dedicated to professional excellence and continuous learning in an environment that values creativity and forward-thinking.

Secureframe is backed by top VCs including Kleiner Perkins, Accomplice, Gradient Ventures (Google’s AI Fund), BoxGroup, Village Global, and many more.

Our product team is looking for strategic problem solvers to take ownership of new products, from discovery to launch and beyond. You’ll work closely with customers and cross-functional teams to explore, iterate, and build the future you strongly believe in.

You’ll own products from conception to launch and be responsible for the development and business success of Secureframe services and products. You’ll get the opportunity to work closely with leadership across the organization. As a member of the Secureframe team, you’ll also influence team culture and how we work cross-functionally together.

We’re a distributed, remote-first company (and have company offsites) that cares about collaboration, ownership, and growth.

Location & Work Model

While Secureframe is a remote-first company, for this role, we have a strong preference for candidates who can work in a hybrid capacity from NYC. Remote candidates are still encouraged to apply

Benefits

  • Medical, dental, and vision benefits for you and your dependent(s)
  • Flexible PTO
  • 401(k)
  • Paid family leave
  • Ground floor opportunity as an early member of the team

What you’ll do

  • Become an expert on the Secureframe product and deeply understand our customers
  • Synthesize customer pain points, user research, analysis, and stakeholder feedback into detailed product requirements
  • Drive feature development from start to finish with the engineering team, while collaborating with key stakeholders across the company
  • Execute on projects and proactively identify improvements and new opportunities for our product
  • Partner with other PMs and cross-functional teams to develop a compelling vision, strategy, and roadmap
  • Work with product and engineering to clarify, triage, and prioritize bugs
  • Assist with projects and go-to-market activities across the product team, including planning, execution, and monitoring success
  • Get savvy with product metrics to assess the health and success of your product

What we’re looking for

  • 5+ years of product management experience, ideally at a B2B SaaS company
  • A builder at heart with strong product taste and a track record of shipping things customers love
  • Rigorous, data-driven approach to prioritization and problem solving
  • Fluent in modern AI tools and excited about what they unlock for how we work and what we build
  • High ownership mentality, you move fast, handle ambiguity well, and don’t need a lot of hand-holding
  • Comfortable operating in a fast-paced, high-growth environment where priorities shift and speed matters
  • Naturally curious and resourceful, with a hunger to keep growing

Secureframe is an equal opportunity employer. We aim to create an environment where every team member at Secureframe feels like they belong so they can have a greater impact on our business and customers. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.

Collaboration, connection, and having fun with colleagues is an important part of our culture as a remote first company.  Therefore, all employees must be able to travel by air to company offsites two to four times per year (reasonable accommodations will be made where appropriate).

We’ve become aware of fraudulent job offers and recruiters falsely claiming to represent Secureframe.

Please note:

1. Official Communication: All genuine Secureframe recruiting communication and job offers are sent from @secureframe.com email addresses.

2. No Fees: We never ask for payments or fees from job applicants at any stage.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

jobs.title: Senior Product Manager
jobs.jd_full:
Who we are:

Financeit is a rapidly-growing fintech and enterprise services company serving some of the largest home improvement and retail organizations in the United States and Canada.

Financeit’s point-of-sale financing platform helps businesses of any size close more sales by offering customers affordable monthly payment options for their next big home improvement, vehicle or retail purchase. For larger enterprise businesses, Financeit delivers an integrated suite of home improvement solutions, known as Centah, that includes leads and workflow management, live contact agents, financing and promotions, performance assurance and category development.

Financeit is small enough that you can make an impact within the company, and large enough to make an impact in the market. As focused as we are on creating unique, category-defining products, we are also building a distinct and inclusive culture.

About the role:

As the Senior Product Manager at Centah, your goal is to drive the development and success of our products. In this pivotal role, you will lead cross-functional teams to define, design, and deliver innovative solutions that meet the needs of our clients and users while driving business growth.

Reporting to the Director of Product, you will define and communicate a clear product feature vision and roadmap aligned with our business objectives, staying ahead of industry trends to identify new opportunities. Acting as the voice of the customer, you will foster a culture of innovation and ensure our products meet real market needs. This role requires strong product judgment, cross-functional leadership, and deep domain expertise in the home services industry.

What you’ll do:

  • Drive the product roadmap for your specific domain, expertly balancing near-term feature delivery with long-term platform scalability.
  • Communicate clearly with senior leaders, presenting insights, tradeoffs, and recommendations that inform strategy and drive company objectives.
  • Act as the ultimate voice of the customer by applying a curious, problem-first mindset to deeply explore root pain points before collaborating on solutions.
  • Champion projects from ideation to launch, meticulously defining requirements, finding the edge cases, and owning the final delivery and results.
  • Lead development teams in an Agile environment through continuous delivery, organizing estimations, refining scope, and making smart trade-offs to meet deadlines.
  • Prioritize product features based on market demand, strategic goals, and robust risk assessment to maximize business impact.
  • Monitor product performance metrics after release, making data-driven decisions to iterate and continuously improve the product.

What you’ll need to succeed:

  • Minimum of 5+ years of experience in product management, business analysis, technical project/program management or software development delivery.
  • Direct experience building out complex web/SaaS/enterprise platforms.
  • Proven ability to thrive in both the strategic and tactical, building a broad vision for a product area while flawlessly executing the plan.
  • Exceptional written communication and story-telling skills.
  • Strong detail orientation with a meticulous approach to developing requirements and identifying edge cases.
  • Experience championing projects from start to finish, communicating the value to both internal and external stakeholders, mitigating risk and owning the delivery/results.
  • Demonstrated ability to act as a self-managed leader who can take ownership of a large scope while remaining highly collaborative with cross-functional pods and technical experts.
  • Expertise with common software development tooling like Jira and Figma.

Winner of Canada’s Most Admired Corporate Cultures for two consecutive years. We offer more than just the basics, take advantage of:

  • An award-winning culture with a collaborative & inclusive team.

  • Competitive pay and performance-based bonus.

    • Base salary: $130,000 - $145,000
    • Annual bonus: 20%

  • Committed to flexible work arrangements, offering hybrid workplace options.

  • Comprehensive medical, dental and vision coverage + Lifestyle Account.

  • RRSP Matching and Parental Leave Top UP Program.

  • In office massage & workout sessions.

  • Virtual events such as Lunch & Learns, company parties, fun team activities and charity initiatives.

  • Career learning and development programs.

Next Steps:

If what you just read excites you, we’d like to hear from you! Please submit your application and we’ll contact you if you become selected for a phone interview.

Financeit is an equal opportunity employer. Accommodation is available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process.

jobs.title: Senior Technical Product Manager I
jobs.jd_full:
Why Housecall Pro?

Help us build solutions that build better lives. At Housecall Pro, we show up to work every day to make a difference for real people: the home service professionals that support America’s 100 million homes. We’re all about the Pro, and dedicate our days to helping them streamline operations, scale their businesses, and—ultimately—save time so they can be with their families and live well. We care deeply about our customers and foster a culture where our company, employees, and Pros grow and succeed together. Leadership is as focused on growing team members’ careers as they expect their teams to be on creating solutions for Pros.  We also offer:

  • A generous benefits program that supports the whole you with medical, dental, vision, life, disability, and 401(k)
  • Paid holidays and flexible, take-it-as-you-need-it paid time off
  • Equity in a rapidly growing startup backed by top-tier VCs
  • Monthly tech reimbursements
  • A culture built on innovation that values big ideas, no matter where they come from

As a Senior Product Manager I, Search Platform, you are the lead architect of the infrastructure that powers search experiences across HCP. This is a high-impact platform role where your primary customers are internal domain teams, and your success is measured by the speed and quality with which they can build, ship, and evolve sophisticated search experiences. You operate with a dual perspective: strategizing for an elite developer experience while maintaining a relentless focus on Pro outcomes. You don’t just manage a roadmap; you build the long-term vision for search infrastructure, ensuring it scales, evolves, and remains a competitive advantage for the entire organization. You are a key influencer across the Product and Engineering teams, guiding how we leverage retrieval technologies to solve complex business problems at scale.

Our team is passionate, empathetic, hard working, and above all else focused on improving the lives of our service professionals (our Pros). Our success is their success.

What you do Each Day

  • Strategize and define the long-term vision for search infrastructure at HCP

  • Build and maintain the Search Platform roadmap, balancing foundational reliability with high-leverage new capabilities

  • Identify and execute opportunities to accelerate product velocity across all domain teams

  • Lead decisions regarding infrastructure and internal builds that drive the most significant impacts for our Pros and the business

  • Drive team efficiency and output quality through the strategic use of AI tools and automation to compress discovery and coordination

  • Analyze performance, latency, and search quality to proactively mitigate risks and surface platform-wide improvements

  • Define and evolve the technical contracts between the platform and domain teams (i.e. schemas, field standards, APIs…)

  • Streamline workflows for schema changes and re-indexing to minimize friction and operational risk

  • Partner deeply with Engineering on index design, data pipelines, search APIs, and observability

  • Architect abstraction layers that enable domain teams to build search experiences without requiring deep infrastructure expertise

Qualifications

  • 5+ years of product management experience, specifically within platform, infrastructure, or developer-facing products

  • Advanced experience in product management for search systems (i.e. OpenSearch, Elasticsearch, or similar…)

  • Proven track record of defining APIs, SDKs, or internal platform contracts consumed by multiple engineering teams

  • Strong analytical skills with the ability to define, monitor, and report on platform health and performance metrics

  • SaaS product experience, ideally supporting business-critical workflows where search is a primary user action

  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent work experience

What will help you succeed in this role

  • Proven experience driving team efficiency or output quality through the strategic use of AI tools and automation

  • Genuine motivation to champion the needs of Search Platform’s direct customers (i.e. internal product and engineering teams)

  • Experience with hybrid search architectures combining keyword/vector search with AI or LLM-based retrieval (i.e. RAG, semantic search, re-ranking)

  • Familiarity with applied ML or LLM integration patterns in production environments

  • Ability to operate strategically while unblocking complex tactical infrastructure hurdles

  • Clear, persuasive communication style capable of making complex infrastructure tradeoffs understandable for all stakeholders

  • Deep commitment to connecting platform investments directly to Pro workflows and outcomes

Founded in 2013, Housecall Pro helps home service professionals (Pros) streamline every aspect of their business. With easy-to-use tools for scheduling, dispatching, payments, and more, Housecall Pro enables Pros to save time, grow profitably, and provide best-in-class service.

Housecall Pro’s brand portfolio includes Business Coaching by Housecall Pro, a business coaching solution for home services businesses. Our brands are united by a singular mission to champion our Pros to success.

We support more than 40,000 businesses and have over 1,800 ambitious, mission-driven, genuinely fun-loving teammates across the globe. If you want to do work that impacts real people, supported by a team that will invest in you every step of the way, we’d love to hear from you

Housecall Pro celebrates diversity and we are committed to creating an inclusive environment.  We are an equal opportunity employer and do not discriminate on the basis of gender, race, religion, national origin, ethnicity, disability, gender identity/expression, sexual orientation, veteran or military status, or any other category protected under the law. #LI-Remote

Location Dependent Information

This role is open to candidates and the expected salary range for this role is $119,000-$149,000. The specific salary for the successful candidate will be determined based on permissible, non-discriminatory factors such as skills, experience, and geographic location. This role is also eligible to participate in Housecall Pro’s equity plan and the following benefits: health care insurance (medical, dental, vision, disability), employee assistance program, 401(K), flexible time off, paid parental leave, tech reimbursement, and other company benefits. Housecall Pro is growing fast and we’re scaling our team to help enable and accelerate our growth.

Privacy Notice for California Job Candidates - Housecall Pro

jobs.title: IT Service Delivery Manager
jobs.jd_full:
IT Service Delivery Manager

VGW is an interactive entertainment company, harnessing technology and creativity to deliver world-class, free-to-play online social games.

We have an exciting opportunity to join our Technology team in Perth and are currently looking for an IT Service Delivery Manager to join the team.

As a key leadership figure, you will provide global direction for our IT Service Delivery team, ensuring seamless operational execution and driving the transition toward a consistent, global service model.

Key responsibilities will include:

  • Leading end-user compute services across our global locations, ensuring top-tier connectivity, support, and hardware management.
  • Transitioning our regional IT experiences into a unified, high-performing global service model and centralised service catalogue.
  • Proposing and executing a transformation plan to align our team structures with our long-term strategy.
  • Managing multi-year device lifecycle budgeting and exploring strategic third-party partnerships for global hardware fulfilment.

What you will bring to the role:

  • At least 5+ years of experience managing large, remote, and culturally diverse IT teams in a global capacity.
  • A proven ability to lead teams through rapid organisational change with high resilience and strategic maturity.
  • A deep understanding of SaaS technology and modern IT infrastructure, ideally backed by an ITIL certification.
  • Experience moving beyond day-to-day ‘firefighting’ to implement proactive scaling, process optimisation, and data-driven dashboards.

About VGW

VGW is an interactive entertainment company, harnessing technology and creativity to deliver world-class, free-to-play online social games.

Our high-quality, acclaimed brands include Chumba Casino, Global Poker and LuckyLand Slots, which are enjoyed by millions of players in North America.

Our games are based on a freemium model, meaning they are available to play at no cost, but include optional in-game purchases to enhance gameplay. We also offer sweepstakes promotions where players can collect free entries for the chance to win real-world prizes.

At VGW, one of our core values is “our players come first”. This means always striving to deliver a safe, responsible and positive player experience.

Why VGW?

At VGW, we’re more than just a team. We’re a diverse and inclusive group of individuals, each playing a vital role in our success.

Our core values are clear and strong; we win together, do what’s right, put our players first and are powered by passion.

We believe in our people and are committed to creating an environment where everyone feels welcome, inspired, and supported. We recognise that life is more than just work - that’s why we’re all about flexibility so you can strike a healthy balance between your personal life and career. We’re also here to champion your growth in your current role and future aspirations, providing opportunities for learning and career advancement. And to top it off, we back this all up with competitive compensation, perks and benefits.

But it’s not all business. We inject fun into the workplace, celebrate together and create lasting memories, from a mix of in-person and online events, to social gatherings and much more.

It’s our combined skills, ideas and visions that drive us forward. Join us on this exciting journey, and together, we’ll achieve remarkable things.

If you want to join a team that does things differently, apply today - we look forward to seeing what you can bring to our team.

Find out more at www.vgw.co

#LI-TP1

#LI-Hybrid

Privacy Policy

jobs.title: Senior Project Manager
jobs.jd_full:
Job Description

The Senior Project Manager is a seasoned professional responsible for leading ITS’s most complex, strategic, or high-risk projects. With a proven track record of managing multi-phase or multi-location engagements, the Senior PM is trusted with clients and initiatives where clear communication, deep technical understanding, and high judgment are critical to success.

In addition to managing project delivery, the Senior PM supports department improvement by mentoring junior PMs, contributing to best practice documentation, and participating in cross-departmental strategy.

Job Responsibilities

Project Delivery & Leadership

  • Own the full lifecycle of high-value or technically complex projects — from scoping and kickoff through deployment and closeout.
  • Lead project teams across departments, managing communication, execution, and stakeholder alignment.
  • Serve as the escalation point for client-facing challenges and scope management during delivery.

Client Engagement & Strategy

  • Build strong relationships with client stakeholders and internal sponsors to align technical execution with business outcomes.
  • Represent the Project department in client-facing meetings, strategy calls, and executive updates as needed.
  • Anticipate client needs and proactively identify project opportunities or risks.

Team & Process Development

  • Mentor and support less experienced PMs through shadowing, knowledge sharing, and peer reviews.
  • Help maintain and improve project documentation standards, templates, and tool usage (e.g., PPM tools, ConnectWise, ITGlue).
  • Participate in quarterly process reviews or department-led improvement initiatives.

What Success Looks Like

  • Senior PM leads projects that are critical to business success and client satisfaction.
  • Project delivered with in scope, budget and timeframe.
  • Scope change is managed effectively through out the life cycle of the project
  • Escalations are limited or none at all.
  • Adherence to company standard project methodology

Job Qualifications

  • Prior experience in a Managed Service Provider (MSP) environment is essential.
  • 10+ years of experience in project management roles, with direct oversight of technical or IT infrastructure projects.
  • Must have hands-on experience with projects including domain migrations, cybersecurity deployments, wireless and network setups, infrastructure rollouts, new client onboarding, and complex client offboarding initiatives.
  • Proven success leading multi-phase or cross-departmental engagements with budget, scope, and timeline responsibility.
  • Strong communication, negotiation, and client-facing skills — especially under pressure or in ambiguous situations.
  • Fluency with project management tools, Microsoft 365, and documentation tools.
  • Ability to coordinate across departments and align project execution with client goals.
  • PMP + or equivalent, ITIL, or Agile/Scrum certifications

Preferred Experience

  • Familiarity with service delivery KPIs and resource planning in a project-based services company.

Job Requirements

  • A home office set up that meets the following:
    • Computer: A working computer with a minimum of 8GB RAM and one of the following:
      • Windows 11 with an Intel i5 or i7 (8th generation or newer), or
      • AMD Ryzen 5 or 7 (2000 series or newer), or
      • macOS 12 or newer.
    • Internet: Stable internet connection with at least 5 Mbps download and upload speeds.
    • Audio Equipment: A quality headset with a noise-cancellation feature for meetings.
    • Mobile Device: A smartphone capable of installing the Microsoft Authenticator app.
    • Webcam: An HD webcam for video calls.
  • A very strong cultural fit the ITS way (Our core values will be discussed during the interview)
  • Excellent command of the English language both spoken and written
  • Able to work full time 40 hours a week:  8:00 AM to 5:00 PM PST (Monday to Friday)
  • Able to pass our online behavior and cognitive assessments.
  • Able to provide NBI Clearance (for PH applicants).

Compensation

Pay rate starts at $14/hour and vary by experience and location.

Benefits

  • Comprehensive Health Plans / HMO (Employee & Dependent/s)
  • Life Insurance
  • Flexible Benefit Reimbursement (Dental/Vision/Mental Health/Wellness/Government Contributions)
  • Paid Time Off (starts at 15 days per year)
  • Maternity/Paternity Leave
  • Paid US Holiday
  • Night Shift Bonus
  • Salary Advancement/Loan
  • Health & Wellness Program
  • Company-paid training and certification

jobs.title: Project Manager
jobs.jd_full:
iKnowHow Group is a leading Software & Robotics Solutions group of companies operating internationally for over 24 years, with 300+ professionals delivering innovative technology solutions across Energy, Telecommunications, Banking & Financial Services, and Public Sector industries. The group is structured into specialized subsidiaries, each focused on distinct technology domains and market verticals.

iKnowHow S.A., as the cornerstone of the group, utilizes state-of-the-art technologies and handles end-to-end internal and external project development, from conception to completion. Our portfolio spans Data & AI platforms, enterprise integration, cloud-native applications, and digital transformation initiatives for major organizations in private and public sectors. We are seeking a motivated and detail-oriented Project Manager (Healthcare) to join our growing team and support the delivery of innovative healthcare and digital transformation projects. The ideal candidate will manage end-to-end project execution, ensuring alignment between clinical/business stakeholders and technical teams, while delivering high-quality solutions within scope, time, and budget.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead the planning, execution, and delivery of healthcare-related projects (e.g., hospital systems, digital health platforms, regulatory initiatives).

  • Act as the primary point of contact between business stakeholders (clinical, operational) and IT teams.

  • Define project scope, objectives, timelines, and deliverables in collaboration with stakeholders.

  • Manage project plans, risks, issues, and dependencies, ensuring timely resolution and escalation when needed.

  • Facilitate workshops, steering committees, and status meetings, providing clear updates on progress, risks, and KPIs.

  • Ensure proper requirements alignment, working closely with Business Analysts and technical teams.

  • Monitor project performance using appropriate tools and methodologies (Agile, Waterfall, or hybrid).

  • Support change management and user adoption, including training coordination and communication plans.

  • Ensure compliance with healthcare regulations, data privacy (e.g., GDPR), and quality standards.

  • Maintain comprehensive project documentation and reporting.

  • Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Healthcare Management, Business Administration, or a related field.

  • 3–5 years of experience in project management, preferably within healthcare, healthtech, or life sciences environments.

  • Proven experience delivering IT or digital transformation projects.

  • Strong understanding of project management methodologies (Agile, Scrum, Waterfall, or hybrid).

  • Experience working with cross-functional teams, including business and technical stakeholders.

  • Familiarity with healthcare systems (e.g., EHR/EMR, hospital information systems) is highly desirable.

  • Strong organizational, problem-solving, and stakeholder management skills.

  • Excellent communication skills in both business and technical contexts.

  • Hands-on experience with project management tools (e.g., Jira, MS Project, ServiceNow) is a plus.

  • Certifications such as PMP, PRINCE2, or Agile/Scrum are considered an advantage.

Competitive salary package based on experience and qualifications

Hybrid/remote working model

Opportunity to work on impactful healthcare transformation projects

Continuous learning and professional development

Collaborative and dynamic work environment

jobs.title: Senior Technical Program Manager
